overview: Generates a report of potential inconsistencies by comparing source and target segments across multiple input documents. It checks both directions: different translations for the same source text, and different sources for the same target text. The XML report groups findings by "key text" with all corresponding variants for easy review.

checkInconststencies.help: Master switch that enables or disables the inconsistency check. Useful for temporarily disabling this step in a pipeline without removing it.

checkPerFile.help:
Processes each input file individually instead of comparing segments across all files.

By default, segments are compared across **all** input documents, which requires holding all segments in memory. Enabling this option reduces memory usage at the cost of only detecting inconsistencies within a single file.
notes: Select this option if you experience memory issues when processing a large number of segments.

outputPath.help: Full path of the XML report file to generate.
outputPath.values: Supports the variables `${rootDir}` and `${inputRootDir}` in the path.
examples: `${rootDir}/inconsistency-report.xml` â€” default, writes to the root directory
`${inputRootDir}/qa/inconsistencies.xml` â€” writes to a `qa` subdirectory relative to the input root

displayOption.help:
Controls how inline codes are rendered in the generated report.

- **Original codes** â€” displays inline codes as they appear in the input documents
- **Generic markers** â€” replaces inline codes with generic numbered markers (e.g. `<1>`, `</1>`, `<2/>`)
- **Plain text** â€” strips all inline codes from the report
displayOption.values: One of: `generic` (Generic markers), `original` (Original codes), or `plain` (Plain text).

autoOpen.help: Automatically opens the generated report file in the system's default application after processing completes. Useful for interactive use in Rainbow or similar tools.

processingNotes: Inline codes are **not** taken into account when comparing segments for inconsistencies.
Text units or segments with no target are excluded from the check.
Text units or segments that are empty **are** included in the check.
limitations: May run out of memory when processing a large number of segments across many files. Use the per-file option to mitigate this.

examples.title: Cross-file inconsistency check
examples.description: Default configuration that checks for inconsistencies across all input documents and generates a report with generic inline code markers.
